div1.entryFree: Elder, subst., the plant Sambucus: my heart of e. Wiv. II, 3, 30 (i. e. weak, faint; spoken to Caius who does not understand it). Judas was hanged on an e. LLL V, 2, 610 (according to a tradition generally received). that's a perilous shot out of an e. gun, H5 IV, 1, 210 (i. e. doing little hurt).* let the stinking e., grief, untwine his perishing root with the increasing vine, Cymb. IV, 2, 59. cf. Elder-tree.
